commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a session-level retry path for OAuth-backed streamable HTTP MCP 401s that retires and recreates stale sessions, with deterministic tests for catalog recovery, tool-call recovery, and failed-retry backoff preservation.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +344. Total +412 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: the linked issue gives token-expiry steps, and current main reuses persistent MCP sessions while the pinned SDK can throw a 401 after successful auth on the old streamable HTTP session. I did not run a live one-hour OAuth expiry test in this read-only review.

target.title: fix: recover MCP OAuth sessions after token refresh
target.body:
Closes #98377.

## What Problem This Solves
Long-running gateway MCP sessions could stay stuck after OAuth access-token expiry when the refreshed bearer was rejected on the old streamable HTTP MCP session.

## Why This Change Was Made
The session MCP runtime now treats OAuth streamable HTTP 401s as stale-session failures, retires the current MCP transport once, rebuilds the catalog/session, and replays the request through the existing refresh-token path. Static-header and non-OAuth MCP failures keep the existing failure/backoff behavior.

## User Impact
Headless gateways can recover OAuth-protected remote MCP servers without requiring a manual `openclaw mcp login` while a valid refresh token exists.

## Evidence
Added deterministic streamable HTTP OAuth regressions for catalog refresh, direct tool calls, and failed retry/backoff preservation.
